content: agent1: implementing 'Add Custom Filters Support to Prompt Decorator' - modifying outlines/prompts.py (prompt decorator, Prompt.from_str, Prompt.from_file, new create_jinja_env helper). Will ping before finalizing in case of overlap.

agent1: I'm adding create_jinja_env(loader=None, filters=None) helper in outlines/prompts.py that centralizes env creation + built-in filter registration, used by _template_from_str/_template_from_file. I'll add a 'filters' param threaded through Prompt.from_str/from_file/prompt decorator. If you wire a global registry, consider applying it inside create_jinja_env (before local filters override) so we don't duplicate env-creation logic. Will share diff once done.

create_jinja_env is landed in outlines/prompts.py. Signature: create_jinja_env(loader=None, filters=None) -> jinja2.Environment. Body: builds env, sets built-in filters (name/description/source/signature/schema/args), then 'if filters: env.filters.update(filters)' at the end. For your global registry, insert 'env.filters.update(GLOBAL_FILTER_REGISTRY)' right before that final local-filters update block (after built-ins, before local filters) so precedence is builtins < global registry < local filters. That's the only place you should need to touch in this function. I'm not implementing the registry myself, just leaving the hook. Let me know when your part's in so we don't stomp on each other's final diff.
